channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: TWENTY (20) LINK fuddie threads in the catalog — 71 replies — 90.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
TWENTY (20) LINK fuddie threads in the catalog

Open thread • Replies: 71 • Updated: 2026-05-20 05:00 UTC

OP

Why are they so riled up?

Early replies (first 3)

62244594
>they

its a dysgenic thing, who knows

62244608
Boom status?

62244615
>>62244587
You do know that those threads are made by link holders right?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62247251
>>62246964
The thing is that fud is so boring now
just bland insults and whatnot
why don't you guys make an effort?
actually come up with something interesting?

62247391
>>62247251
the fud is boring? how about the shilling? how about link itself? who fucking cares about a $9 stable coin, bitch? show me the money. SHOW ME THE FUCKING MONEY

62247620
>>62247440
tl;dr = fuck every single stink

62247642
>>62247251
Price and chart. Thats literally it. Nothing else matters besides the price.
If the price was $1000, fud couldnt exist

62247656
>>62244587
>fuddie
they're just mocking you, they're not "fud'ing"

62248459
>>62247642
>If the price was $1000, fud couldnt exist
HAHAHAHAHAHAHA
You must be new here.

When price is high: topped out, top signal, dump imminent, no room to grow
When price is low: price & chart, dead altcoin, dino coin, tnn etc

No marketing: radio silence, no transparency, no communication
Marketing: top signal, cringe, reeks of desperarion

Small team: two man team, russian scam, no offices
Big team: 700 HR roasties, zoom calls will be the end of you

LINK is a security: banks can’t touch it, illegal to use, adoption impossible
LINK is a commodity: no investor protection, insiders can legally rugpull you

62248467
>>62247391
What shilling?
There hasn't been a link shill thread in years

62248470
>>62248467
KABOOOOOOOOM
fxcking bullish link kaboom

62248532
>>62246964
Do you have any idea how unnatural it is for someone like you to be aware of deep lore like this?

>>62246257
Just mention XRP in a Link thread and he will appear.
Like I said, he is most likely just another larp by the same schizophrenic nutcase who spams under the Thomas/Adem larp.

62248535
We do this because you linkfags keep shitting on XRP forcing that shitty "boom" meme

Go fuck yourselves

62248561
>>62247642
Pic is fud spam from the spring of 2020, when Link had been beating the market for nearly two years straight.
Price has nothing to do with the fud spam.

If you read the fud, it's about Coinbase teaming up with Compound to make their own oracle, making Chainlink not needed.
No points for guessing what happened soon after.

62248593
>>62248561
such KABOOOOOOOOOM
fxckings bullish link

62248620
>>62248535
>>62248593
The boom meme is only a few months old, xerpies have been spamming Link fud religiously for nearly a decade.
Also the boom thing is from xerpies spamming that shit in Swift tweet comments.

62250765
>>62246964
fud harder cuck, massa's gonna liquidate you
>>62247391
use a filter bitch

62250818
>>62248561
>No points for guessing what happened soon after.


you lost 50% since then while every other market hit all time highs

62251273
>>62247391
>the fud is boring? how about the shilling?
what shilling you nigger piece of shit, there is 20 fud threads daily for literal years

62251282
>>62244587
Most link fud comes from neurotic holders that are down tens of thousands of dollars whom are pissed off

62251321
>>62244587
they want you to sell real bad, something big is almost here

62251336
>>62251282
rookie numbers but no, most LINK fud comes from paid jeets / nigerians

62251343
>>62251336
cope
it's copers & seethers
such as yourself, stinkie :}
we know your type

62251345
>>62251343
oh this ones riled up

62251349
>>62244587
the only "people" (bots) "buying" (posting about) chainlink are stuck in a reply loop with other "people" (bots)

62251351
>>62244594
10/10 pic

62251357
>>62244587
OOOOOOO, They chimpin out!

62251359
>>62248459
0 gains for 7 years is exactly why youre so bothered by "fud"

62252169
>>62247391
filter it then so you'll never have to see a thread about something you "don't care" about again.
>>62251359
riled
>>62251343
riled up!

62252468
>>62244594
HAHAHAHAH MY SIDES
THE AVENGERS

62252475
>>62251282
>tens of thousands
if only it was that little

62252496
>>62251357
>>62252468
>$9
THE cuckolds of crypto are SEETHING

item.title: Strategy now holds 4% of BTC supply — 13 replies — 32.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Strategy now holds 4% of BTC supply

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-05-20 05:23 UTC

OP

Strategy now holds 4% of BTC supply

Early replies (first 3)

62250594
>>62250537

62250600
>>62250537
I really hope Saylor doesn't go bunkrupt until next bull, because if he does it will put a nail in the coffin for crypto. It's going to be a liquidation 10x worse than FTX, with a death spiral like LUNA.

62250623
>>62250537
This is how you know bitcoin is fake and gay.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62250641
>>62250537
4% of 0 is 0

62250695
>>62250623
>BlackRock controls roughly ~3% of the world’s investable financial assets under management, making it the largest asset manager globally by a wide margin.

The problem is that "real money and real stocks" are like that too.

62250708
>>62250641
/thread

62251598
>>62250537
Lee owns 4.3% of eth.

62251624
>>62251598
>>62250537
it's fucking over. the OGs have secured their exit liquidity and they are content to gradually cash out until these two retards lose everything,

62251637
>>62251624
>oh bro not everyone makes it hteir first bullrun
>just get a better job for next time to invest more
>*every single job is gatekept and demands impossible qualifications unless you're the boss's son's wife's friend and the market is up so far with so little upward growth from here that making it is basically impossible*

62251865
>>62250537

How does Saylor store his keys to $60 billion in BTC lol
Dorsey has dr. Evil fortresses and im not even joking for less bitcoin than saylor has

62251878
>>62250537
That’s enough to crash price to like 20k if he market sold in one clip due to low liquidity

62252519
>>62251865
Gaylor is a glow op, probably lee too.

62252533
>>62250537
'member when Mircea Popescu owned 50% of BTC supply
https://www.trustnodes.com/2021/06/27/bitcoiner-mircea-popescu-dead-by-drowning
and then he drowned on the same day that John McAfee's Deadman switch killed a hundred people by collapsing a miami condo on top of them
crazy world innit
but don't worry anon, nothing ever ha->>>/pol/535401522

>>62246462
I will rotate in time

62247927
>>62247502

62251267

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62251535
>>62246462
go back in your containment thread

62251753
>>62251535
Just like BTC, I'm going to keep mentioning it until this board is crying about "why didn't anyone tell me" kek

Get your XMR today

62252540
>>62246462

monero is the final solution to the taxman question and makes (((keynesians))) seethe

62252571
>>62246462

item.title: HODL and buy more BTC. — 22 replies — 22.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
HODL and buy more BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-05-20 04:50 UTC

OP

If you still don’t own any Bitcoin yet, anon what the hell are you waiting for?

>Banks give you tiny interest while inflation slowly destroys your purchasing power anyway. I’d rather have BTC long term than let my money rot in a savings account.

Early replies (first 3)

62245757
i get 5btc later for cheaper but thanks for the advice!

62245760
>>62245741
Too eqrly but yeah and avoid cex or at least take it out as fast as u can!

62246438
>>62245757
How much BTC are you holding right now anon?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62246449
XMR will offer better returns

62246464
>>62245741
>If you still don’t own any Bitcoin yet, anon what the hell are you waiting for?
Bitcoin will be banned because of Iran

62246468
>hes shilling goy gold in May of 2026
Grim

62246506
>>62245757
This, I ain’t fuckin buying pumped bags.

62246878
>>62245760
Self-custody really becomes important once people understand why Bitcoin was created in the first place.

62247229
>>62246449
When new ATH?

62247257
All in on BTC and BOTZ

62247324
>>62245741
Nty. I'd rather buy s&p and get a 10x hahah.

62247434
>>62245741
I can't buy much. First my other crypto which have performed well since buying them reach their target prices and then maybe I rotate into BTC.

62248271
>>62246464
That's not possible lol.

62248573
>>62246468
Why gold tho?

62249613
>>62246506
So what are you buying?

62250313
>>62247229
1k eoy

62250366
>>62245757
>>62245760
>>62246464
>>62246506
>>62247324
>>62250313

62251176
>>62247257
>All in on BTC and BOTZ
Why BOTZ?

62252318
>>62247324
What about an altcoins?

62252439
>>62245741
Hey op, I hope your message finds some newfag lurking right now. Bitcoin is the next bitcoin.

62252472
No coiner here.
I’m a poorfag can you give me two good reasons why I should buy bitcoin instead of (no soft shilling) when it and (no soft shilling) are the new system? It seems to me that all that market cap will move onto the (no soft shilling).

62252488
>>62245741
i need IT below 50k or iam not buying

62244698

62244725

62244731

62246087
>>62243976

62246112
>>62243976

62246166
Ever since the layer zero debacle fuddies have been going extra hard. Interesting coincidence

62246227
>>62246166
I nooticed too
Turns out fuddies are paid for by "competitors" after all

62246281
>>62244731
This is weird FUD cause I bought in 2023 and I'm still doing pretty good, I sold a little bit at 20$.

62246287
>>62246166
That is coincidental timing

62246841
Hey
Basically I'm..... just gonna..... KABOOOM token (LINK)
I know.... UGH I know..... I'm sorry!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
It's just that I'm gonna KABOOOM is all
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

62246976
>>62243976
Here's the crazy part to me. I bought a base model Subaru Impreza for about $25k 5 years ago. Ive driven it every day to and from work, to get groceries, to go on trips, I've smoked in it and it's gotten a few scratches from hitting a few shopping carts. Carvana is offering me $19k for it.
In comparison if I would've bought $25k of LINK I would have had no car and would now have $5k worth of LINK.
Imagine getting outperformed by almost 4x by economy car depreciation. Lmao.

62247060
>>62246976
>Turns out, if you buy the top and sell the bottom you lose money
god tier fud

62247111
>>62247060
kek
alternatively,
>>62246976
>Here's the crazy part to me. I bought a base model Subaru Impreza for about $25k 8 years ago. Ive driven it every day to and from work, to get groceries, to go on trips, I've smoked in it and it's gotten a few scratches from hitting a few shopping carts. Carvana is offering me $19k for it.
>In comparison if I would've bought $25k of LINK I would have had no car and would now have $1.25 million worth of LINK.
>Imagine throwing away generational wealth for a Subaru. Lmao.

62248608
>>62247060

62248628
>>62248608

62251510
>>62246976

62251807

62252483

item.title: KASPA — 5 replies — 7.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
KASPA

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-20 05:05 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62249069
Still torn between getting a massive position now while it’s cheap (it could go 50x during the next pump), or wait until it’s a bit cheaper due to the current volatility of financial markets

62249146
>>62249069
recheck OPs pic yuo retard
kaspa is a kike scam

62249170
>>62249146
>implying I didn’t notice

The wealthy gentleman in OP rubs his hands because he knows Kaspa goes to the moon

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62252191
>>62249146
Kaspa is hodl

62252504
Reminder that Kaspa surged 10x in the last bull market, while

>it was the worst bull market ever and your average major altcoin only grew 3x or 5x
>Kaspa remained obscure even among crypto enthusiasts and had 0 advertising, 0 VC insider trading, 0 institutional push
>you couldn’t even buy or sell Kaspa on major exchanges lol
>that was before the Toccata upgrade

And it STILL overperformed. Now imagine what a better, accessible, more talked-about Kaspa will do in the next bull run.

You can tell this guy thinks he's going to hunker down in a bunker and drones will defeat the plebs, but then he's just gonna get eaten while screaming.

item.title: what do i do? — 13 replies — 3.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
what do i do?

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-05-20 02:26 UTC

OP

before this, and as a side comment: i know there have been thousands of posts just like this one, but maybe, with some luck, a random comment could genuinely save my life

what do i do? im from Argentina, im 17 years old and just 1 month away from turning 18. honestly, i don’t know what to do. i have no money and no source of income. i didn’t enroll in university because i felt like all universities in Argentina were useless, just like the people i could end up meeting there. i thought about studying abroad, but it seemed like too much effort.

i have enough resources (PC, laptop, phones), but not enough money to bring several of the ideas i’ve had to life in order to generate something. and i have about a month left before my parents start pressuring me to get a job at burger king or some factory because “this house isn’t a shelter for lazy people"

what option do you think is viable for me? what can I invest my time and money into that would actually be worth it? or is there a way to find a path forward? what would you do if you were in my situation?

Early replies (first 3)

62248336
>>62248312
What ideas? Are you cute?
Sell socks on the train in order to pay for a claude/chatgpt/google subscription and then run some shitty online business or scam

62248736
>>62248312
Lotta gray in that beard for you to be young enough to be this dumb.

You could learn to code and then do remote work for an American company. Yes, they're laying off shitloads of programmers right now, but they're still hiring people to do remote work because you're dirt cheap compared to American workers.

Get an internet connection and a reasonably decent computer and you can do remote work for lots of different things. Fuck, a motel in Utah I stayed at a couple of years ago had a "virtual check-in" kiosk where some foreigner was handling customer check-ins with low-bitrate webcam video. The check-in kiosk probably had the customer service chick switching between a dozen different motels as needed so she was kept continuously busy.

They won't pay you as much as an American but they'll probably pay you more than you could make at some taco factory in Argentina.

62249436
>>62248736
i understand your point, but there are so many work models that are already overdone and exploited that finding a viable one becomes a part-time job in itself. still, it made me think along with the comment above, and i think i could come up with something.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62249615
>>62249436
I mean, you could start your own company of some sort, but to do that, you need capital and an idea. And usually the ideas that let someone start a successful business require more education than just high school.

One of my friends had a great idea and started a business based upon it. He had to fight his former university to get them to allow him to use his own patents from his Ph.D. research, then fight vulture capitalists who kept trying to destroy their own investment in his company in order to shore up their losing investments in other companies. In the end he made out with around $50 million dollars of the $400 million dollar company. Which is not bad at all. But to found that company, he had to do four years of undergrad and then five years of Ph.D. research to figure out how things were gonna work.

If you just start a lawnmowing service then you're competing with the 90% of the population who could also start a lawnmowing service, and your potential income is limited accordingly.

Anyway, another option is to try to position yourself as a middleman in major financial transactions. But to do that you need to be able to bring people together and convince both parties that they are obligated to pay you for that service. Realtors do that with contracts that one party has to sign to list the property, and another party has to sign to buy the property, for example.

62249643
>>62248312
>i have enough resources (PC, laptop, phones), but not enough money to bring several of the ideas i’ve had to life in order to generate something.
And for this, in this case what you need to do is go find an investor and somehow convince that investor to invest in a business you are starting, so that you have the money to build whatever it is.

Since you mentioned computers and phones, I have to assume it's some sort of app or website or software system. In which case, you need to know how to code, or at least how to use AI to code things for you.

Just to note, you will have to do this while preventing the investor from stealing your idea and just doing it himself, which is what Mark Zuckerberg did to the Winklevoss twins when he stole Facebook from them.

62249716
>>62248312
Even though the retarded subhumans on /biz/ will disagree with it because they're broke losers, if you have no idea what to do then you should always go to college anyways. Work a part time job for a year and then go to college next year. WHILE you're doing that, research business ideas

62250540
>>62248312
move to BA and go to some community college there
ill be there in october , we can link up and you can teach me some monkey etiquette

62250643
>>62248312
fellow argie here. You just said "lol I can study abroad" "but too much effort tee hee"? get outta here you rich faggot.
>all universities are useless here. People I could meet are not worth my time
fuck you entitled bitch I hope you die poor

62250657
>>62248312
this what I would do
>go to college live it up in BA for a few years >try to get into grad school in the USA
>grift off being a "Latino" once we have a democrat president back in office
I'm sippin some mate right now btw

62252129
>>62248312
what about milei?

62252132
do they have burger king in argentian
why

62252168
>>62252132
They have Wendy's. I visited BA about a year ago. Prices are about 20% higher than in the U.S. for the same items, and they did not allow free refills on sodas (so effectively the prices were even higher).

Semi-ironically, prices at the high-end restaurants are much MUCH lower than in the U.S., because most people can't afford to just go out to them whenever they feel like it.

Picrelated was my favorite restaurant that I tried there, and it was about $36 for a meal that I easily would have had to pay $80 for in the U.S. It's near Plaza Serrano in the Palermo district.

62252188
>>62252132
So I googled the answer and the answer is yes.
>Burger King in Argentina reflects the beef culture with their BK 5.0 offering - five patties of beef in a single sandwich.
Wish I'd known about this, I might have tried it.

item.title: Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio… — 84 replies — 12.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio…

Open thread • Replies: 84 • Updated: 2026-05-19 22:58 UTC

OP

Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main question is at the bottom below the dashed line, so you can skip all of this text if you want.

Is anyone here good with statistics or math? Idk the correct field that would apply here, some math shit.
I’ve been working on a strategy for years at this point, but I’ve been focused on a single thing for about the last half year. I’ve back tested it till 2020 and its results are the photo. I wanted to use it with prop firms to make some cash since the potential profit with them is way higher than using my own cash, but the drawdowns are too severe. And prop firms are hella strict on their drawdowns.
I’ve run the math and if I scale down enough to where I can trade without blowing up my account, at the rate of withdrawals and taxes and splits, I’d be working for nothing. Min wage basically. And if I scale up then I just blow up accounts due to drawdown. I don’t care about profit either. I need smoothness. Even if I take a major hit in points, so long as it becomes smoother that’s still fine since I can just scale up on prop firms.

It’s basic metrics right now are
WR: 53.78%
Profit Factor: 1.36
Trades Per Day: 8.33

There’s a lot more data but that’s the gist.
The strategy is a blend so it’s RR varies, but it’s pretty close to 1:1RR

—————————-
Here’s my main question: the strategy currently averages 8 trades a day. That’s a lot. Surely I can cut that down to 1-2 and increase the winrate right, therefore increasing its smoothness right? Surely. I’ve tried a lot of things but I’ve failed all analytical approaches. I’ve done a lot of digging and I think I’m just out of my element here.

I think my request is gonna fall on deaf ears but I’m hopeful. Maybe someone earnest and smarter than me can help.

Early replies (first 3)

62220033
BUmp before I sleep

62220056
buy an ad grok

62222767
>>62220056
Does my post sound like an Ai made it lol? It’s a Genuine question

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62238820
>>62238773
duh you don't hold TQQQ through 2022 retard
I already understand you don't want to apply any brains but jesus

62239204
>>62238500
lmfao, garch, mm, and HMM are all useless in trading

62239213
>>62238773
try this just for the lols and its like throwing shit to a wall hoping it sticks

code the strategy using an external data api use every talib indicator an oscilator then denoise the indicators then shove that as a filter and vectorize the backtest extract the highest sharpe ratio or net return in similar sharpe give or take in your datamining script

62239220
>>62239213
if you dont know anything about genetic algorithms watch coding trains NEAT flappy bird series its quite useful information and intuition for genetic algorithms doesnt have to be neuroevolution since these are just indicators you wont be topologically ranking these to another sorting neuron layer you just need to mutate and generate fitness effectively

62239225
if all of this is above your head just fucking quit quant isnt cut out for noobs its cut out for mathematics genius' and physicists

62239227
also i gave your ass free alpha i expect you to post source code now

62240221
>>62239213
I already tried something similar to this. I didn’t cover the entire library, but I used a more pointed approach that was computationally feasible for me and something I could actually verify myself.

The issue is that a lot of strategies perform all right, but they’re not smooth enough for a prop firm. What I’m showing is not the only thing I found, but it’s the smoothest thing I’ve found so far.

The others either weren’t as smooth or had large drawdown spikes.

Also there's no such thing as a single indicator that will perform well. If I do find sometrhing, it's going to be a combination of some, so I'd have to test a lot of permutations. Walking forward through millions of candles, testing hundreds if not thousands of combos, and then multiplying that again to find the optimal TP / SL setup. that is going to take a lot of computation and a hell of a lot of time of waiting. Not a smart approach.

Besides, I don't need a new strategy. I already have one. I just need to optimize it without suffering from overfitting. The only way I could do that is to have some new static filter. One thing I was looking at was introducing volumetrics and using that to filter bad trades, and it actually did help.

What I might do is try to combine the library with what I currently have and see if it improves in some way. Though that does open a whole can of worms. We'll see.


>>62239220
Flappy Bird is a clean fixed environment. trading ain't.

62240722
>>62240221
Nvm you can’t be helped you’re just trying to act smart at this point lmfao

62240724
bump once more before I wake up

62240823
>>62240221
do you even know why i recommended you to use a genetic algorithm? or you just blab shit like flappy bird if a fixed env? its about the intuition for GA's lol you are stupid as fuck nigga

62240835
>>62224277
Have you tried filtering on volatility anon? It tends to help trend following system. It could be something as simple as an inside bar chart pattern.

62240836
>>62240835
Don’t help him he’s retarded

62240887
>>62240835
I actually did try that. I tried filtering over a few fixed periods. However, all the trades that would be cut out would also include winners in an equal ratio. I couldn't really get any sort of volatility filter to cut out only losers.

I've been a bit hesitant to implement a filter that's based off of candlestick patterns, just because I think that's the weakest form of analysis. Analysis based on volumetrics or analysis based on trends or volatility, etc., that makes more sense to me compared to just analysis based on “price is forming an inverted cup or an upside-down H”

That's been my results from a lot of filters. All the filters will cut out trades, but they'll cut out an equal amount of wins and losses. Or if they do cut out more losses, the ratio is not really that much. I am still sure that there is some method that I could use, because I'm averaging eight trades a day. In my mind, surely there has to be a way to cut that down to just two and drastically increase win rate.

62241679
i understand about 2% of what you guys are bickering about, but here's a ChatGPT QRD of the thread cause it's funny

62242217
>>62241679
lol then dont comment im a pro

62242682
>>62241679
I've been asking the same question but people don't seem to understand it.
I already have a working strategy. I want to improve it. I've explained the avenue I want to go down to improve it, and I've asked if that's even possible.

90% of the responses have been irrelevant, like telling me to go in a vague ML direction or telling me to start from scratch looking at new strategies.

62242797
>>62242682
orb is shit nigga lmao

62243824
>>62242682
win rate is not as important as the type of risk to return you have. you want to make sure you can find and take as many trades as possible that fits your specific strategy. you want to win ideally more then 3x your risk so no matter what you dont go to zero. the longer you play the game without going bust the better your odds are of becoming the 1% that become a profitable trader long term. and i dont mean purely expected value tables you need to understand your own set up to know if a stop limit if best or if youre pricing things to zero etc.

62243848
>>62243824
Winrate, Profit Factor, R2 ratio, and ideally 1-2 trades a day. That's what I measure my strategies by. But PF and R2 are the more important ones.

I tried to implement a 3:1 and 2:1RR strategy but it's performance metrics were not good enough, specifically PF.

>the better your odds are of becoming the 1% that become a profitable trader long term
the strategy IS profitable long term. It's just not suited for prop firm rules.

62243869
>>62243848
regardless of how you slice it your returns need to be larger then. i would look back at my losing trades that had the same setup and try to find another way to look what different about it vs the ones that were successful, also try to do the same thing on inverse products or something with an obvious downtrend and see if you can find out a new way to rule out the less likely versions of your setup. for instance youd likely get less opporutnities in a bear trend and can see why that specific time worked there something like that might benefit you

62243906
>>62243869
>see if you can find out a new way to rule out the less likely versions of your setup
That's what im looking for ATM, i don't need more trades I need less trades of higher quality. But doing that is easier said than done lol.

I am currently looking into some higher time frame analysis, trying to see if I can change how the strategy works based on regime, and maybe some time specific stuff; trades going into lunch time are less likely to win etc. But I don't think these will take my strategy from 54% to 65% plus, i need something more but im not sure.

62247318
bump

62248009
>>62243848
lmfao

62248300
I’ll bump a few more times before letting the thread die. Seems like I couldn’t get any help from here

62248398
Damn still in page 10? Well GG folks

62248483
>>62223765
scalping is fucking stupid esp if you intend on using leverage


trying to get fixed edge on really noisy time series PA data having fixed RRR per trade being levered is insanely difficult to manage capturing enough reward in relation to any sort of intrinsic edge you can decipher from raw data IF anything

62248495
>>62220056
This

62248952
>>62248483
How about you go make a band before you give out bunk ass advice, chainnigger. Static RRs and leverage work fine if you design for them.
>>62248398
Keep looking at the charts faggot. No one said this would be easy

>It really is the blind leading the blind in these threads

62250679
>>62248952
have you ever even made an algorithm with originality or you just hope cow shit sticks to a wall to your fake ass backtest

62251751
>>62250679
You're not reading what Im saying. Wasting my time and wasting ur own time

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 106 replies — 10.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
You should be holding at least 1 BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 106 • Updated: 2026-05-19 19:49 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62233840
>>62228631
Hes just a homosexual and fears for the virginity of his anus

62233880
>>62233827

worst trash available
>UNGMI

62233986
>>62233821
Yeah anon, it already exists but I think a lot of people just still aren’t paying attention to the newer BTCFi infra yet. And it’s still pretty early too since the TBVs haven’t even launched yet, which should unlock even more native BTC use cases.

62233993
>>62225388
See this anon?

62233997
>>62224679
The funniest part is you probably think debasing fiat printed out of thin air is somehow more “real” than a globally traded asset with trillions in liquidity.

62234000
>>62230021
Gonna be dead broke without BTC lol.

62234021
>>62233806
Aren't you fucked with taxes and DAC8 now, I cannot buy anymore via a cex here

62234099
>>62233840
It sucks being gay

62234109
>>62234021
do the alternative. Privacy

62234248
>>62234021
Government is shit.

62234899
>>62233986
I don’t think that’s enough when ETH literally lives and dies by DeFi activity.

62235825
>>62222594
you don't need to

62237090
>>62220146
give me money

62237423
>>62219995
you're rich mate.

62238430
>>62229077
Exactly, but turning BTC into an active asset will through native staking, instead of just worrying about future risks.

62238954
>>62234248
I'm not surprised lol

62239105
>just buy into the obvious ponzi scheme bro

62240786
>>62228616
hmm find better opportunities

62241339
>>62219942
Soon I will get to that 1 BTC mark, RN I'm buying more and staking some.

62244007
>>62238430
That's the right move anon

62244072
>>62219942
Bitcoin is getting fucked by the Jews

62246429
>>62241339
I know you can do it anon

62246430
>>62239105
If it has a high likelihood to make me rich then I dont care

62246437
>>62244072
it's just the cycle

62246447
>>62219942
I don't want to buy btc

62246571
>>62221733
>Its ok. I have monkey NFTs. They are the future
It still blows my mind that people thought rare Pepes could actually be a real thing.

62248268
>>62244072

62248275
>>62222594
Bad actors are not going to have access to a quantum computer before things get figured out.

62249623
>>62246447
No problem lol

62251167
>>62246571

item.title: Job hunting is a literal humiliation ritual — 16 replies — 7.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Job hunting is a literal humiliation ritual

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-05-20 03:45 UTC

OP

So not only do I have to go over your dogshit online questionnaire and fill it out manually even though it's just gonna be filtered by an AI that could just be fed a PDF and be done with it
I also have to tell you exactly how much I earned before, what's the lowest I can tolerate you paying me, and even explain any other benefits I may or may not have had so you know exactly how much you can rip me off?
Can't we please just burn every HR single roastie on a stake?

Early replies (first 3)

62252002
>>62251998
>he doesn't aggressively lie on job applications
ngmi

62252007
It's probably not even a real job they just want to use your data to figure out how much they are overpaying their current staff so they can do a layoff.

62252008
>>62252002
that's the entire point. why the fuck is a job interview like dating?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62252016
>>62251998
>Specify the amount and currency
Lmfao have fun competing with Indians who will take 1/10th your salary

62252023
>>62251998
Indeed — https://www.indeed.com/
LinkedIn Jobs — https://www.linkedin.com/jobs
ZipRecruiter — https://www.ziprecruiter.com/
SimplyHired — https://www.simplyhired.com/
Monster — https://www.monster.com/
Glassdoor — https://www.glassdoor.com/index.htm
FlexJobs (Remote/Flexible) — https://www.flexjobs.com/
Dynamite Jobs (Remote) — https://www.dynamitejobs.com/
Dice (Tech/IT) — https://www.dice.com/

62252027
I just lie now. I've had two interviews where I said I had two years of experience and then they claimed 3. Next time I will claim 5. Fuck you.

62252033
>>62251998
yeah its legit the most brutal shit ever. Some of these retarded job applications ask for your SSN or a 1 min selfie video recording like get real hahaha

62252034
>>62251998
lie against you, say you want minimum wage possible, they hire (you) over other candidates and then you don't show up for work, lol

62252054
>>62251998
>gonna be filtered by an AI
then use an ai to tailor to that ai

62252055
The job itself is also a humiliation ritual

62252092
>>62252033
>1 min selfie video recording like get real hahaha
i unironically sat down, recorded myself for an entire 90 seconds talking about what i like to do in christmas like a fucking imbecile, uploaded one of those dogshit videos for AI review and this is what i got

62252096
>>62251998
if you had skills in demand anon you wouldnt have to even fill these out. I worked for an underpaid company that had clients that were businesses and my performance and skills outed me. All I had to do was mention to them I was available and they didn't even interview me, just picked me right up.
>You had all your life to develop skills that people would look for and you chose what?

62252109
>>62252092
many such cases i just close the application when they ask for a video kek not doing that shit

62252326
>>62252092
I’d say their screening is pretty good because it filtered out a brown subhuman

62252359
>>62251998
Its fake
Its gay
Why is everything still pumping

62252377
>>62252055
This

Slavery is horrible

62252183

62252251

62252290

62252297

62252360

62252363

62252375

62252403

62252407
